name: Ehukai Pillbox (Sunset)
tagline: Watch pros surf Pipeline from a WWII bunker on the ridge — short, steep, and spectacular.
parking: Street parking on Ke Nui Rd near Sunset Elementary
bestTime: Golden hour / sunset for best light; winter mornings to watch Pipeline surf from above
talkStory: These concrete bunkers were built in 1943 as part of Oahu's coastal defense network after Pearl Harbor — the Army placed pillboxes along every ridgeline with ocean sightlines to watch for Japanese invasion. During winter big-wave season, locals hike up with binoculars to watch the Pipe Masters from above, a perspective no beach spectator gets. The second pillbox at the summit gives you a bird's-eye view stretching from Ka'ena Point to Kahuku.
